name: ActiveJ Specializer
summary: ASM-driven runtime specialisation: takes an instance with predictable state, rewrites it as a static class with all field accesses inlined as constants. ~7× speedup recovering manual-code performance. JVM-only. The concept generalises: when an object's state is effectively immutable, specialise it as code.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
ActiveJ Specializer is a runtime instance-specialisation library: it takes an instance whose fields are effectively immutable (configuration objects, dependency-injection-bound services), generates a new class via ASM where the field values are inlined as constants, and replaces the original instance with one of the generated class.

The payoff: every `instance.someField` access in user code becomes a constant load (or, after JIT, simply a constant in the generated machine code). For configuration-heavy code paths (frameworks, DI containers, query engines), this can be substantial.

sections.title: Design pattern — runtime specialisation as library
sections.content:
Reported speedup of ~7× on the benchmark workloads ActiveJ uses internally for dependency injection bootstrap and query-engine setup. The trade-off is JVM-only and requires the instance state to be predictable (changing field values during the program's run breaks the specialisation invariant).

The broader pattern — "when an object's effective state is fixed, specialise it as static code" — appears in other forms across the industry: V8's hidden-class transitions specialise on object shape; LLVM's interprocedural constant propagation specialises functions on constant arguments. ActiveJ Specializer makes the technique explicit and accessible as a library on the JVM.
